algae_description: Dense green surface coverage — likely frogbit, duckweed, or floating plants blanketing nearly the entire water surface. Some green growth also visible on substrate in the lower left, possibly a small stem or moss plant establishing.
substrate_condition: Dark granular substrate (appears to be aqua soil or similar), generally clean. A mesh media bag — likely containing crushed coral or aragonite for buffering — rests prominently on the substrate near center.
concerns: Surface plant coverage is extremely dense — nearly 90%+ of the water surface is covered by floating plants, which could significantly restrict gas exchange and reduce light penetration to mid/lower tank plants
Water has a faint haze or cloudiness — could be a bacterial bloom consistent with active nitrogen cycling on Day 39, worth monitoring
Condensation/water droplets on the inner rear glass suggest high humidity at the surface — consistent with dense surface coverage trapping moisture
narrative: Day 39 of the cycle and the tank is unmistakably alive — one shrimp is visible mid-water, a vivid red streak against the hazy column. The floating plant layer has gone full canopy, blanketing nearly the entire surface, and a crushed coral (or similar) media bag sits squarely on the substrate, doing quiet buffering work. The water carries a faint milkiness that fits the profile of a bacterial bloom mid-cycle.
